channel.title: Science & Nature News
channel.description: Latest news from BBC Sky at Night Magazine, BBC Science Focus, and Discover Wildlife

item.title: Aliens may be rare because coal is rare. Scientists say fossil fuels may be vital for technological civilisations to emerge
item.description: Is technology on alien worlds reliant on fossil fuels?

item.title: Watch live views of Earth and the Moon from NASA's Artemis II spacecraft as it zooms through space
item.description: How to watch the livestream from the Artemis II capsule showing real-time views of Earth and the Moon during the mission

item.title: Stargazing | What to see in the night sky this month
item.description: What can you see in the night sky tonight? Find out what's on view in our monthly stargazing guide.

item.title: Visible planets in the night sky, April 2026
item.description: Your guide to seeing the planets in 2026

item.title: Venus is back! And it's only going to get better. How to see the Evening Star throughout April
item.description: A guide to observing the Evening Star this month

item.title: To boldly go... There was a bit of toilet trouble on Artemis II, but it's now been resolved, says NASA
item.description: Houston, we have a (toilet) problem... And we fixed it. Here's how the crew of Artemis II tackled the problem.

item.title: Artemis II launches to the Moon. Here's how to track it on your smartphone
item.description: How to keep track of the Artemis II mission in real time

item.title: 'And lift off!' Watch the moment NASA's Artemis II rocket launches, taking astronauts to the Moon
item.description: Watch a video of NASA's Artemis II rocket launching from Kennedy Space Center. Catch the 10-second countdown and lift-off.

item.title: Artemis II successfully launches on its journey to the Moon. A new era in crewed spaceflight has begun
item.description: The Artemis II mission is on its way to the Moon

item.title: Artemis II will broadcast 4K from the Moon. Laser tech could give us Netflix-style views of the lunar surface
item.description: NASA's O2O system is capable of sending down 4K high-definition video from the Moon

item.title: Saturn’s moon Titan may hold the building blocks of alien life in its methane lakes and rivers
item.description: Study suggests that life’s chemical precursors could arise in non-water environments

item.title: The cosmic explosion that wouldn't quit. Strange signal from deep space baffles astronomers
A real-life Tatooine? Astronomers discover trio of planets in rare double-sun system
item.description: Get the key facts on this story with our quick, 1-minute read Star system: astronomers have discovered three Earth-sized planets orbiting a binary star system known as TOI-2267 Close proximity: the system is located 190 lightyears from Earth, which is considered relatively nearby in astronomical terms Binary nature: the two stars in the system are cool and orbit each other at a distance just eight times the distance between Earth and the Sun Record breakers: this discovery represents the coldest and most compact pair of stars known to host planets Transit first: the study marks the first time planets have

item.title: The US is now paying more than any other country for climate change damage, study suggests
item.description: Despite being the biggest carbon emitter, the US is already paying a disproportionate price for the climate crisis

item.title: The 5 biggest myths about taking statins to lower cholesterol
item.description: Statins save lives – so why are so many people wary of taking them?

item.title: I'm a neuroscientist. Here's all you need to know about money to be truly happy
item.description: We all need enough funds to cover our basic needs, but beyond that the connection between wealth and wellness is less clear

item.title: There's no such thing as a shark, suggest scientists
item.description: Analysis of shark DNA has thrown a wrench into our understanding of these sharp-toothed predators

item.title: We’re closer than ever to bringing back life from cryogenic freezing
item.description: Astronauts in cryosleep is one of science fiction's most enduring fantasies – but a new study suggests the gap between fiction and reality just got a little smaller

item.title: An ancient Maya ‘codex’ keeps predicting future events with eerie accuracy
item.description: The Maya civilisation is known for its art and architecture. But as we decipher their surviving cultural relics, we’re discovering Maya astronomy was even more advanced than we had imagined

item.title: The unsettling reasons bowel cancer might be rising among under-50s
item.description: Deaths from the main types of cancer in people under 50 are falling. But for bowel cancer, they’re increasing

item.title: This (very weird) rule has been controlling fashion for 150 years
item.description: Ever wondered why low-rise jeans are making a comeback?

item.title: Scientists want to create ‘T. Rex’ quakes on the Moon
item.description: Artificial Moonquakes on demand could help scientists investigate below the lunar surface

item.title: Your most draining relationships (except one) are taking years off your life, study suggests
item.description: Difficult people don’t just zap your energy – they may also accelerate your biological ageing

item.title: We've found a bizarre alien magma planet surprisingly close to Earth
item.description: The floor (and the rest of this planet) is lava

item.title: Just one day in your life could soon reveal how long you’ll live
item.description: After building a Truman Show experiment, scientists were able to predict the lifespans of their 81 subjects

item.title: This stunning 3D map lets anyone explore real human organs down to a single cell
item.description: It’s far less gross than it sounds (we promise) and could have major implications for how we understand anatomy and disease

item.title: This terrifying new dinosaur could rewrite the origins of T. rex
item.description: The emergence of the world’s most iconic predator remains a scientific mystery. This latest discovery offers a critical clue

item.title: ‘Stunning’ new treatment may reverse frailty after just one dose
item.description: A landmark trial suggests stem cells can reverse the physical decline of ageing

item.title: Even experts can't tell if these faces are AI-generated or not. Can you?
item.description: Even ‘super recognisers’ of human faces struggle – can you do better?

item.title: Scientists spot signs of cannibal killer whales in Russian waters
item.description: Two severed fins bearing the tooth marks of other killer whales have raised a troubling question: are some orcas hunting their own kind?

item.title: Scientists have found a dinosaur ‘Rosetta Stone’ hidden for 90 million years
item.description: A near-complete fossil from Patagonia has finally cracked open the mysterious evolutionary story of one of prehistory's strangest dinosaur groups

item.title: ‘Blood rain’ could sweep across the UK this week as Saharan dust plume moves in
item.description: Vivid sunsets can also be expected

item.title: Scientists could soon use giant fire tornadoes to clean up our oceans
item.description: Sometimes, the craziest ideas just happen to be the best

item.title: Some (very weird) weapons are being launched in space. And that's only the beginning
item.description: From lasers to space planes, the battle for space is already underway – and everyone wants to win it

item.title: Something very strange is happening under Greenland’s ice
item.description: A scientist described the phenomenon as “like a pot of boiling pasta”

item.title: These (adorable) seal pups chat just like humans – accents included
item.description: They're seal-iously good conversationalists

item.title: Birdwatching could help slow ageing, breakthrough study finds
item.description: It turns out being bird-brained could actually be the ultimate goal for your cognitive health

item.title: For 50 years, there have been rumours of these fish climbing waterfalls. Scientists just filmed them doing it for the first time
item.description: Remarkable footage shows hundreds of shellear fish scrambling up Luvilombo Falls in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.

item.title: Researchers tracked 9 great hammerhead sharks off Florida and the Bahamas. Here's what they discovered
item.description: Scientists wanted to learn more about how the largest hammerhead species on the planet may cope as the climate changes.

item.title: Scientists looked inside a bee hive. What they saw is remarkable
item.description: Researchers wanted to learn more about the insect's famous 'waggle dance'. Here's what they found.

item.title: Great crested grebe seen drowning and eating chiffchaffs in Spain
item.description: Birdwatcher and photographer Juan Montiel captured the unusual grebe behaviour on camera.

item.title: This baby elephant taking its first trembling steps is the cutest thing you’ll see today
item.description: Baby elephants learn to walk within the first hour of their life. If they didn’t, they might not survive.

item.title: There's a tropical island in the middle of the Panama Canal that's home to a bright pink animal with an astonishing trick
item.description: Researchers on Barro Colorado Island say the animal changes colour from pink to green. And they think they know why

item.title: "Exceptional discovery." Animals thought extinct for 6,000 years found alive in Indonesian rainforest
item.description: Two marsupial species previously only known from the fossil record have been rediscovered in New Guinea.

item.title: “It’s a bit like sea turtle forensics." Scientists looked inside 24 turtles – and uncovered secrets from the past
item.description: Sea turtle shells are helping researchers learn more about major environmental changes in the ocean's past.

item.title: Strange organism that looks like a tongue found in English woodland. It has never been seen before in the UK
item.description: The rare blue-based earthtongue fungus was discovered at Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve in West Sussex.

item.title: Scientists tracked 39 polar bears and 26 seals through the Arctic wilderness. What they discovered is important
item.description: The researchers wanted to learn more about how climate change is reshaping Arctic food webs.

item.title: Biologists stuck a camera on a sea turtle – and it filmed this
item.description: TurtleCams are helping marine researchers learn more about the lives of sea turtles as they cruise through our seas.

item.title: 'As we approached the overhang, we spotted it – a bizarre creature with a fleshy beard lurking in the shadows'
item.description: Wobbegong sharks are ambush predators, hiding in reefs waiting for their prey to swim close enough. But they're very hard to find, says Melissa Hobson, who was lucky enough to spot one on a recent dive in Indonesia.

item.title: Snoozing polar bears among 5 stunning images from Wildlife Photographer of the Year People's Choice Award
item.description: Wildlife Photographer of the Year Nuveen People’s Choice Award winners announced, featuring sleepy polar bears, graceful flamingos and a very playful lynx.

item.title: Researchers put a spy camera into the ocean. And this spiny creature took the bait
item.description: This predatory starfish can grow to 70cm in diameter and has white spines all over its body.

item.title: Drones film sperm whales headbutting each other. It's the first time the behaviour has been caught on camera
item.description: Marine scientists have filmed and scientifically described sperm whales headbutting each other for the first time – validating what mariners have said since the 19th century.

item.title: “Its prey was still alive.” Photographer captures blue dragons feeding in unbelievable detail
item.description: A nature photographer in Gran Canaria captured hypnotic close-up footage of blue dragon nudibranchs feasting on prey.

item.title: "I would wonder if I was just seeing things.” Scientists make illuminating discovery in South Carolina swamp
item.description: Researchers in Congaree National Park have uncovered how fireflies synchronise their bioluminescent light shows.

item.title: 100km-wide "hidden giant" discovered beneath Antarctic ice sheet
item.description: A vast granite body roughly half the size of Wales has been found beneath the Pine Island Glacier in West Antarctica…

item.title: Photographer spots snow leopard in remote Indian mountains. He wasn’t expecting it to do this
item.description: Life can be tough for these rare predators, but they still find time to enjoy themselves...

item.title: Scientists record world's longest underwater timelapse. What is reveals is astonishing
item.description: The 1,000-day underwater timelapse, recorded on an urban reef in Miami, shows corals bleaching before making a miraculous recovery.

item.title: Real-life possum found hiding in soft-toy display at Tasmanian airport
item.description: Passengers and staff spotted the curious creature in a gift shop at Hobart Airport.

item.title: Ginormous king penguin colony filmed on remote Atlantic island. The sound of it is ear-splitting
item.description: Listen to the remarkable sound of thousands of penguins calling to each other in South Georgia.

item.title: Florida's 'spinning' sawfish were in trouble. Now, scientists think they may have hope
item.description: An area of the Indian River Lagoon in Florida may be providing suitable habitat for juvenile smalltooth sawfish, suggesting there is hope for the recovery of the critically endangered species.

item.title: Yellowstone received a whopping 4,762,988 visits in 2025. But it wasn't the most-visited US national park
item.description: People flock to Yellowstone for its dramatic geysers, unique geological features and incredible wildlife. You may (or may not) be surprised to learn that it was not the most-visited US national park in 2025.
